nodes.context: International confraternity with roots in Nigeria, engaged in cybercrime, election fraud, human trafficking, drug trafficking, money laundering, and murder.
Original confraternity from which Black Axe was formed; a male brotherhood focused on Pan-African struggles.
Rival confraternity to Black Axe, involved in violent clashes.
Rival confraternity to Black Axe, involved in a fight in May 2009.
Rival confraternity involved in a fight in March 2010 resulting in nine deaths.

edges.evidence_quote: Originally formed from and inspired by the Neo Black Movement of Africa (NBM)
edges.context: Black Axe was originally formed from and inspired by the Neo Black Movement of Africa.

edges.evidence_quote: At least 53 people were injured or killed between March 2009 and June 2013 in fights between the Black Axe and their rivals, the Eiye confraternity.
edges.context: Black Axe and Supreme Eiye Confraternity engaged in violent fights resulting in casualties.

edges.evidence_quote: In May 2009, there was a fight between the Black Axe and the De Norsemen Kclub of Nigeria.
edges.context: Black Axe and De Norsemen Kclub of Nigeria fought in May 2009.

edges.evidence_quote: In March 2010, another fight broke out against the Maphite confraternity, resulting in the deaths of nine people.
edges.context: Black Axe fought Maphite Confraternity in March 2010, resulting in nine deaths.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Supreme Eiye Confraternity, De Norsemen Kclub of Nigeria, and Buccaneers Confraternity
edges.context: Buccaneers Confraternity is listed as a rival of Black Axe in the infobox.
